events: FOMC meeting July 28-29; rate decision Wed July 29 at 2:00 PM ET
Peak Q2 earnings season: Tesla and Alphabet reported after close July 22; Intel, Honeywell, Blackstone report Thu July 24
ADP private-hiring slowdown data released this week
Next CPI release not scheduled this week (last was July 14)
oil_brent: ~$98.22 (up ~34% in July; ceasefire collapsed, tanker attacks in Strait of Hormuz)
sp500_forward_pe: 20.3x (above 5-yr avg 19.9x and 10-yr avg 19.0x, but down 7.7% YTD on strong earnings growth)
investor_sentiment: bearish (AAII bulls fell from 44.9% to 29.6%; bears rose to 42.3%)
note: Cautious posture maintained and reinforced. The US-Iran ceasefire that briefly calmed markets has collapsed; three tankers attacked in the Strait of Hormuz; Brent crude up ~34% in July to ~$98. The 10-year Treasury yield is at a two-month high (~4.64%), raising borrowing costs and inflation concerns ahead of the July 29 FOMC decision. The S&P 500 forward P/E of 20.3x is above both the 5-year and 10-year averages. Futures are pointing lower on a mix of higher yields, elevated energy prices, and earnings disappointments (Tesla, Alphabet both fell post-report). None of our three thesis candidates (MSFT, V, AAPL) trade below even a normal-posture margin-of-safety threshold. A cautious posture demands an even wider discount before any buy. Hold cash; no new positions today.
